Records.description: Three-loop banana in $D=2$ with pairwise-equal internal masses ($m_1=m_3$ and $m_2=m_4$). The period vector is four-dimensional $(\Pi_2, \Pi_{12}, \Pi_{11}, \Pi_0)$. After a $GL(4,\mathbb{Z})$ change of basis the transcendental lattice becomes $T = H \oplus \langle 2 \rangle \oplus \langle -6 \rangle$. Via the exceptional isomorphism $O(2,2) \sim SL(2,\mathbb{R}) \times SL(2,\mathbb{R})$ restricted appropriately, the holomorphic period and mirror map are Hilbert modular forms on $H \times H$.
Records.familyName: three-loop banana (pairwise-equal masses, Hilbert modular) / three-loop banana with pairwise-equal masses (Hilbert modular periods)
Records.method: K3 periods, transcendental lattice analysis, GL(4,Z) basis change, Hilbert modular forms, mirror map
Records.location: section 'Automorphic properties of three-loop banana integrals', subsection 'The pairwise-equal-mass case m1=m3 and m2=m4' (bananas_2.tex)

Records.description: Three-loop banana integral family with two distinct internal masses in the 2-2 configuration: two propagators carry mass $m_1$ and two carry mass $m_2$. Nine denominators (four propagators + five ISPs). Computed in $D=2-2\varepsilon$ dimensions. The underlying geometry is a K3 surface (Calabi-Yau 2-fold) with two moduli $z_1=m_1^2/p^2$, $z_2=m_2^2/p^2$. Eight master integrals: two tadpoles, four in the derivative block on the maximal cut, and two decoupling integrals. The canonical differential equation is derived locally at the MUM-point $(z_1,z_2)=(0,0)$ with integer coefficients. This is the first epsilon-factorised form for this integral.

Names: three-loop banana with pairwise-equal masses (Hilbert modular periods)
